title: Final Peer Set for Deep-Dives
methodology: Merged quantitative driver rankings (VD-A5) with qualitative strategy profiles (VD-B1) and action catalogs (VD-B2) to select a final set of 12 firms for deep-dive analysis. Inclusion criteria: (1) auto-include firms in top quartile on 2+ drivers; (2) include cautionary cases in bottom quartile with well-documented strategic actions; (3) flag non-obvious peers from A5; (4) require minimum 2 sources per included firm.

final_set.inclusion_rationale: Ultimate scale leader — Q1 on management fee revenue ($7.1B, rank 1/21). Largest listed alt manager. Sets the benchmark for scale premium in valuation multiples. Rich qualitative profile with 5 documented strategic actions (VD-B2). P/FRE 25.9x.
final_set.deep_dive_focus: How does BX leverage scale to command premium multiples? What role do perpetual capital vehicles (BREIT, BCRED, BxSL) and the private wealth channel ($260B) play in driving durable fee growth?

final_set.inclusion_rationale: Q1 on credit exposure (75%) and permanent capital (65%). Highest P/DE disparity (P/FRE 30.3x vs P/DE 13.9x) — Athene insurance platform inflates DE well above FRE. Key model for PAX's credit expansion aspirations. Rich qualitative profile with 4 documented actions.
final_set.deep_dive_focus: How does APO's integrated insurance-asset management model (Athene) create a permanent capital flywheel? What lessons for PAX in building credit/insurance-linked strategies in LatAm?

final_set.inclusion_rationale: Q1 on 3 drivers: management fee revenue ($3.7B), FRE margin (65%), and FRE growth (37%). Demonstrates how a legacy PE franchise diversifies into credit and insurance (Global Atlantic) while maintaining margin leadership. 4 documented actions in VD-B2.
final_set.deep_dive_focus: How did KKR execute the Global Atlantic insurance acquisition and integrate it into its fee-earning model? What drove KKR's industry-leading FRE growth (37% YoY)?

final_set.inclusion_rationale: Q1 on management fee revenue ($3.4B) and FRE margin (73% — highest in sample). Pure-play AM spin-off from Brookfield Corporation, demonstrating the 'capital-light, pure-fee' model. Q2 on permanent capital (50%). Relevant as a potential structural analog for PAX if PAX separates its GP from its balance sheet.
final_set.deep_dive_focus: How does BAM achieve 73% FRE margin? What operating model efficiencies drive the industry's highest margin? How does the BAM/BN spin-off structure create valuation advantages?

final_set.inclusion_rationale: Q1 on management fee revenue ($3.7B) and credit exposure (70%). The largest credit-focused listed alt manager. Demonstrates how a credit-first strategy scales rapidly while maintaining high fundraising momentum. 4 documented actions in VD-B2.
final_set.deep_dive_focus: How does ARES leverage its credit-dominant franchise to drive scale and fundraising? What explains the low FRE margin (41.5%) despite massive scale? Is the credit model inherently lower-margin than PE?

final_set.inclusion_rationale: Q1 on 4 drivers: FRE margin (59%), credit exposure (55%), permanent capital (91%), and FEAUM growth (25%). The most 'permanent capital intensive' listed alt manager. Unique GP stakes strategy provides diversification. 4 documented actions in VD-B2.
final_set.deep_dive_focus: How does OWL achieve 91% permanent capital? What does the GP stakes model teach about alternative revenue diversification? Why does OWL trade at a discount (P/FRE 15.5x) despite leading on multiple drivers?

final_set.inclusion_rationale: Cautionary case — bottom quartile on FEAUM growth (-0.9%) and permanent capital (20%), but demonstrated massive FRE margin improvement (+900bps YoY to 46%) through cost discipline. Q1 on FRE growth (30%). Documents what happens when a legacy PE franchise faces growth challenges and how cost restructuring can preserve/boost FRE despite flat AUM. 3 documented actions.
final_set.deep_dive_focus: What drove CG's FRE margin expansion (+900bps) despite flat FEAUM? What operational changes did Harvey Schwartz implement? How does CG's cost restructuring playbook apply to smaller managers like PAX?

final_set.inclusion_rationale: Q1 on management fee revenue ($2.5B). Europe's largest listed PE-focused alt manager. Highest EV/FEAUM in sample (21.7%) — demonstrates the 'European PE premium.' Slow FEAUM growth (4.6%) and FRE growth (4.3%) but maintains premium valuation due to franchise strength and carry optionality. 4 documented actions in VD-B2.
final_set.deep_dive_focus: Why does EQT command the highest EV/FEAUM (21.7%) in the sample despite modest growth? What role does carry optionality play? How does EQT's European institutional base compare to PAX's LatAm LP base?

final_set.inclusion_rationale: NON-OBVIOUS PEER — Q1 on 3 drivers: FRE margin (72.3%), FEAUM growth (49%), FRE growth (100%). Not in initial B0 peer set. European mid-market specialist that IPO'd in 2021 and doubled AUM through ECP acquisition. Closest structural analog to PAX's M&A-driven growth trajectory. Trades at P/FRE 7.9x — deep discount to megacaps despite strong metrics.
final_set.deep_dive_focus: BPT shares PAX's profile: mid-scale, recently public, acquisition-driven growth, high FRE margin. Why does BPT trade at an even deeper discount than PAX (P/FRE 7.9x vs 12.3x)? What explains the European small-cap PE manager discount? Is the ECP acquisition integration a template for PAX?

final_set.inclusion_rationale: NON-OBVIOUS PEER — Q1 on FEAUM growth (50%) and FRE growth (40%). IPO'd April 2024 on Euronext Amsterdam. The most recent large alt manager IPO, providing a real-time case study on post-IPO valuation dynamics. Significantly larger than PAX ($1.4B fees) but with similar high-growth, diversified characteristics.
final_set.deep_dive_focus: How has CVC's post-IPO valuation evolved? What drove 50% FPAUM growth (organic + DIF acquisition)? How does CVC's credit and secondaries diversification compare to PAX's strategy?

final_set.inclusion_rationale: Cautionary/comparator — Q1 on FRE margin (59%) but bottom quartile on multiple growth metrics. Pure-play infrastructure GP with no credit, no permanent capital, and single-asset-class concentration (HHI 10,000). Provides contrast to PAX's diversified model. Demonstrates how an infrastructure-focused GP can maintain high margins but limited growth optionality.
final_set.deep_dive_focus: What are the valuation consequences of single-asset-class concentration? How does ANTIN's P/FRE (9.9x) compare to PAX (12.3x) given similar scale but different diversification? Does PAX's multi-asset approach justify a premium?

final_set.inclusion_rationale: Closest scale comparator to PAX — similar management fee revenue ($291M vs PAX $298M), similar FEAUM ($25.7B vs $33B). Q1 on permanent capital (85%). Trades at P/FRE 6.4x — the deepest discount in the sample. Provides a controlled comparison: similar scale, different geography and strategy.
final_set.deep_dive_focus: PX is nearly identical in scale to PAX but trades at a 48% discount (P/FRE 6.4x vs 12.3x). What explains this gap? Is it PX's lower-middle-market PE/VC focus? Lack of credit? US-only geography? PX provides a natural experiment for isolating the 'LatAm premium/discount' in PAX's valuation.

excluded_candidates.exclusion_rationale: Only Q1 on zero drivers. Moderate multiples (P/FRE 22.6x). Interesting Angelo Gordon credit acquisition story, but less directly comparable to PAX than included peers. Can be added in a Phase 2 deep-dive if needed.

Q1 on FEAUM growth only. Private markets solutions provider — different business model (fund-of-funds, co-investments) from PAX's direct investment model. Qualitative profile in B1. Could be included for its private wealth channel insights.
Q1 on FRE growth (34%) only. Similar to STEP — solutions provider model, less directly comparable to PAX. March fiscal year complicates timing alignment.
No Q1 appearances on any driver. Smallest P/FRE in the pure alt category (13.8x). Interesting as a hedge fund allocator but model is too different from PAX's direct investment approach.
No Q1 appearances. European credit-focused manager with moderate growth. FRE margin (33%) is the lowest in sample. Less directly relevant than other European peers (BPT, CVC, ANTIN).
No Q1 appearances. Eurazeo's hybrid model (balance sheet + AM) makes valuation comparisons difficult. Interesting transition story but lower priority than included peers.
Traditional/multi-boutique AM, not a pure alternative manager. Low EV/FEAUM (3.2%) reflects traditional AM model economics, not comparable to PAX.
Missing most valuation metrics (no P/FRE, no P/DE). European credit specialist — would be interesting for credit comparison but insufficient data for quantitative analysis.
Excluded at VD-A3 stage — quant/hedge fund model incompatible with alt-manager valuation framework.
Excluded at VD-A3 stage — does not report FRE/DE/FEAUM in comparable format.
Excluded at VD-A3 stage — proprietary capital investor, not a fee-earning AM.
Excluded at VD-A3 stage — transitional FRE ($27M trailing), P/FRE not meaningful.
Excluded at VD-A3 stage — hybrid mortgage REIT + AM, Sculptor segment not isolable.
Excluded from correlations — BN market cap includes non-AM businesses. BAM (pure-play AM spin-off) is included instead.
